title: When Noticing a Purpose in Random Events: - Ask Yourself:
subtitle: Question Purposeful Explanations
description:
When noticing a purpose in random events:
- Ask yourself: "Is there any actual evidence of intention or design here?"
- Consider randomness: Some things happen by chance without any goal behind them.
- Separate cause from purpose: Look for causal explanations rather than assuming intent.

Example: A storm ruined plans? Don’t assume it “happened for a reason”—explore natural causes instead.

howTo.title: Pause and Reflect
howTo.summary: When you notice a purpose in a random event, pause for 60–90 seconds. Ask yourself if there is any actual evidence of intention or design behind the event.

howTo.title: List Expected Evidence
howTo.summary: Create a mini-list of up to three pieces of evidence you would expect if there were intention. This helps shift your focus from narrative to concrete facts.

howTo.title: Choose an Action
howTo.summary: Based on your evidence check, decide on an immediate action. This could be to fix, accept, or ask for more information regarding the situation.

howTo.title: Log Your Findings
howTo.summary: Record your pause, evidence list, and action in a journal or app. This helps track your progress and reinforces the habit over time.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reflect on your experiences. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/question-purpose-in-random-events/.
